methods.description: This method involves compiling pollen records from various sites to reconstruct vegetation responses to climate changes during the Pleistocene-Holocene transition. The analysis helps understand how vegetation and climate influenced human activity in the region.

steps.description: Vegetation responses to climate changes around the time of the Pleistocene-Holocene transition have been reconstructed by compiling pollen records from sites located between 46 and 50 S in the Andean region and Deseado Massif in Santa Cruz Province.
steps.searchable_summary: compile pollen records from specified sites for vegetation reconstruction

steps.description: The interpretations of the fossil sequences are based on comparison with present-day pollen assemblages from different vegetation communities.
steps.searchable_summary: compare fossil pollen sequences with present-day assemblages

steps.description: Representative variables of the main vegetation units at each site, obtained through pollen analysis, are plotted in Fig. 3.
steps.searchable_summary: plot representative variables of vegetation units

steps.description: Based on these data and on the interpretation of the sequences, vegetation was reconstructed for the periods 13-11 cal ka BP, 11-10 cal ka BP, and 10-8 cal ka BP.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct vegetation for specified periods based on pollen data
